1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم والذاريات ذروا
1.english: By those [winds] scattering [dust] dispersing
1.transliteration: Waz-zaariyaati zarwaa

1.arabic: فالحاملات وقرا
1.english: And those [clouds] carrying a load [of water]
1.transliteration: Falhaamilaati wiqraa

1.arabic: فالجاريات يسرا
1.english: And those [ships] sailing with ease
1.transliteration: Faljaariyaati yusraa

1.arabic: فالمقسمات أمرا
1.english: And those [angels] apportioning [each] matter,
1.transliteration: Falmuqassimaati amraa

1.arabic: إنما توعدون لصادق
1.english: Indeed, what you are promised is true.
1.transliteration: Innamaa too'adoona la-saadiq

1.arabic: وإن الدين لواقع
1.english: And indeed, the recompense is to occur.
1.transliteration: Wa innad deena la waaqi'

1.arabic: والسماء ذات الحبك
1.english: By the heaven containing pathways,
1.transliteration: Wassamaaa'i zaatil hubuk

1.arabic: إنكم لفي قول مختلف
1.english: Indeed, you are in differing speech.
1.transliteration: Innakum lafee qawlim mukhtalif

1.arabic: يؤفك عنه من أفك
1.english: Deluded away from the Qur'an is he who is deluded.
1.transliteration: Yu'faku 'anhu man ufik

1.arabic: قتل الخراصون
1.english: Destroyed are the falsifiers
1.transliteration: Qutilal kharraasoon

1.arabic: الذين هم في غمرة ساهون
1.english: Who are within a flood [of confusion] and heedless.
1.transliteration: Allazeena hum fee ghamratin saahoon

1.arabic: يسألون أيان يوم الدين
1.english: They ask, "When is the Day of Recompense?"
1.transliteration: Yas'aloona ayyaana yawmud Deen

1.arabic: يوم هم على النار يفتنون
1.english: [It is] the Day they will be tormented over the Fire
1.transliteration: Yawma hum 'alan naari yuftanoon

1.arabic: ذوقوا فتنتكم هذا الذي كنتم به تستعجلون
1.english: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."
1.transliteration: Zooqoo fitnatakum haa zal lazee kuntum bihee tas ta'jiloon

1.arabic: إن المتقين في جنات وعيون
1.english: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and springs,
1.transliteration: Innal muttaqeena fee jannaatinw wa 'uyoon

1.arabic: آخذين ما آتاهم ربهم ۚ إنهم كانوا قبل ذلك محسنين
1.english: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.
1.transliteration: Aakhizeena maaa aataahum Rabbuhum; innahum kaanoo qabla zaalika muhsineen

1.arabic: كانوا قليلا من الليل ما يهجعون
1.english: They used to sleep but little of the night,
1.transliteration: kaanoo qaleelam minal laili maa yahja'oon

1.arabic: وبالأسحار هم يستغفرون
1.english: And in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness,
1.transliteration: Wa bilashaari hum yastaghfiroon

1.arabic: وفي أموالهم حق للسائل والمحروم
1.english: And from their properties was [given] the right of the [needy] petitioner and the deprived.
1.transliteration: Wa feee amwaalihim haqqul lissaaa'ili walmahroom

1.arabic: وفي الأرض آيات للموقنين
1.english: And on the earth are signs for the certain [in faith]
1.transliteration: Wa fil ardi aayaatul lilmooqineen

1.arabic: وفي أنفسكم ۚ أفلا تبصرون
1.english: And in yourselves. Then will you not see?
1.transliteration: Wa feee anfusikum; afalaa tubsiroon

1.arabic: وفي السماء رزقكم وما توعدون
1.english: And in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised.
1.transliteration: Wa fissamaaa'i rizqukum wa maa too'adoon

1.arabic: فورب السماء والأرض إنه لحق مثل ما أنكم تنطقون
1.english: Then by the Lord of the heaven and earth, indeed, it is truth - just as [sure as] it is that you are speaking.
1.transliteration: Fawa Rabbis samaaa'i wal ardi innahoo lahaqqum misla maa annakum tantiqoon

1.arabic: هل أتاك حديث ضيف إبراهيم المكرمين
1.english: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham? -
1.transliteration: Hal ataaka hadeesu daifi Ibraaheemal mukrameen

1.arabic: إذ دخلوا عليه فقالوا سلاما ۖ قال سلام قوم منكرون
1.english: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace, [you are] a people unknown.
1.transliteration: Iz dakhaloo 'alaihi faqaaloo salaaman qaala salaamun qawmum munkaroon

1.arabic: فراغ إلى أهله فجاء بعجل سمين
1.english: Then he went to his family and came with a fat [roasted] calf
1.transliteration: Faraagha ilaaa ahlihee fajaaa'a bi'ijlin sameen

1.arabic: فقربه إليهم قال ألا تأكلون
1.english: And placed it near them; he said, "Will you not eat?"
1.transliteration: Faqarrabahooo ilaihim qaala alaa taakuloon

1.arabic: فأوجس منهم خيفة ۖ قالوا لا تخف ۖ وبشروه بغلام عليم
1.english: And he felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not," and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.
1.transliteration: Fa awjasa minhm khee fatan qaaloo laa takhaf wa bashsharoohu bighulaamin 'aleem

1.arabic: فأقبلت امرأته في صرة فصكت وجهها وقالت عجوز عقيم
1.english: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"
1.transliteration: Fa aqbalatim ra-atuhoo fee sarratin fasakkat wajhahaa wa qaalat 'ajoozun 'aqeem

1.arabic: قالوا كذلك قال ربك ۖ إنه هو الحكيم العليم
1.english: They said, "Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo kazaaliki qaala Rabbuki innahoo huwal hakeemul 'aleem

1.arabic: ۞ قال فما خطبكم أيها المرسلون
1.english: [Abraham] said, "Then what is your business [here], O messengers?"
1.transliteration: Qaala famaa khatbukum ayyuhal mursaloon

1.arabic: قالوا إنا أرسلنا إلى قوم مجرمين
1.english: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals
1.transliteration: Qaalooo innaaa ursilnaaa ilaa qawmim mujrimeen

1.arabic: لنرسل عليهم حجارة من طين
1.english: To send down upon them stones of clay,
1.transliteration: Linursila 'alaihim hijaa ratam min teen

1.arabic: مسومة عند ربك للمسرفين
1.english: Marked in the presence of your Lord for the transgressors."
1.transliteration: Musawwamatan 'inda rabbika lilmusrifeen

1.arabic: فأخرجنا من كان فيها من المؤمنين
1.english: So We brought out whoever was in the cities of the believers.
1.transliteration: Fa akhrajnaa man kaana feehaa minal mu'mineen

1.arabic: فما وجدنا فيها غير بيت من المسلمين
1.english: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims.
1.transliteration: Famaa wajadnaa feehaa ghaira baitim minal muslimeen

1.arabic: وتركنا فيها آية للذين يخافون العذاب الأليم
1.english: And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.
1.transliteration: Wa taraknaa feehaaa aayatal lillazeena yakhaafoonal 'azaabal aleem

1.arabic: وفي موسى إذ أرسلناه إلى فرعون بسلطان مبين
1.english: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.
1.transliteration: Wa fee Moosaaa iz arsalnaahu ilaa Fir'wna bisultaa nim mubeen

1.arabic: فتولى بركنه وقال ساحر أو مجنون
1.english: But he turned away with his supporters and said," A magician or a madman."
1.transliteration: Fatawalla biruknihee wa qaala saahirun aw majnoon

1.arabic: فأخذناه وجنوده فنبذناهم في اليم وهو مليم
1.english: So We took him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea, and he was blameworthy.
1.transliteration: Fa akhaznaahu wa junoo dahoo fanabaznaahum fil yammi wa huwa muleem

1.arabic: وفي عاد إذ أرسلنا عليهم الريح العقيم
1.english: And in 'Aad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.
1.transliteration: Wa fee 'Aadin iz arsalnaa 'alaihimur reehal'aqeem

1.arabic: ما تذر من شيء أتت عليه إلا جعلته كالرميم
1.english: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.
1.transliteration: Maa tazaru min shai'in atat 'alaihi illaa ja'alat hu karrameem

1.arabic: وفي ثمود إذ قيل لهم تمتعوا حتى حين
1.english: And in Thamud, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."
1.transliteration: Wa fee Samooda iz qeela lahum tamatta''oo hattaa heen

1.arabic: فعتوا عن أمر ربهم فأخذتهم الصاعقة وهم ينظرون
1.english: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.
1.transliteration: Fa'ataw 'an amri Rabbihim fa akhazal humus saa'iqatu wa hum yanzuroon

1.arabic: فما استطاعوا من قيام وما كانوا منتصرين
1.english: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.
1.transliteration: Famas tataa'oo min qiyaaminw wa maa kaanoo muntasireen

1.arabic: وقوم نوح من قبل ۖ إنهم كانوا قوما فاسقين
1.english: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.
1.transliteration: Wa qawma Noohim min qablu innahum kaano qawman faasiqeen

1.arabic: والسماء بنيناها بأيد وإنا لموسعون
1.english: And the heaven We constructed with strength, and indeed, We are [its] expander.
1.transliteration: Wassamaaa'a banainaa haa bi aydinw wa innaa lamoosi'oon

1.arabic: والأرض فرشناها فنعم الماهدون
1.english: And the earth We have spread out, and excellent is the preparer.
1.transliteration: Wal arda farashnaahaa fani'mal maahidoon

1.arabic: ومن كل شيء خلقنا زوجين لعلكم تذكرون
1.english: And of all things We created two mates; perhaps you will remember.
1.transliteration: Wa min kulli shai'in khalaqnaa zawjaini la'allakum tazakkaroon

1.arabic: ففروا إلى الله ۖ إني لكم منه نذير مبين
1.english: So flee to Allah. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
1.transliteration: Fafirrooo ilal laahi innee lakum minhu nazeerum mubeen

1.arabic: ولا تجعلوا مع الله إلها آخر ۖ إني لكم منه نذير مبين
1.english: And do not make [as equal] with Allah another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
1.transliteration: Wa laa taj'aloo ma'al laahi ilaahan aakhara innee lakum minhu nazeerum mubeen

1.arabic: كذلك ما أتى الذين من قبلهم من رسول إلا قالوا ساحر أو مجنون
1.english: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."
1.transliteration: Kazaalika maaa atal lazeena min qablihim mir Rasoolin illaa qaaloo saahirun aw majnoon

1.arabic: أتواصوا به ۚ بل هم قوم طاغون
1.english: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they [themselves] are a transgressing people.
1.transliteration: Atawaasaw bih; bal hum qawmun taaghoon

1.arabic: فتول عنهم فما أنت بملوم
1.english: So leave them, [O Muhammad], for you are not to be blamed.
1.transliteration: Fatawalla 'anhum famaaa anta bimaloom

1.arabic: وذكر فإن الذكرى تنفع المؤمنين
1.english: And remind, for indeed, the reminder benefits the believers.
1.transliteration: Wa zakkir fa innaz zikraa tanfa'ul mu'mineen

1.arabic: وما خلقت الجن والإنس إلا ليعبدون
1.english: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
1.transliteration: Wa maa khalaqtul jinna wal insa illaa liya'budoon

1.arabic: ما أريد منهم من رزق وما أريد أن يطعمون
1.english: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.
1.transliteration: Maaa ureedu minhum mir rizqinw wa maaa ureedu anyyut'imoon

1.arabic: إن الله هو الرزاق ذو القوة المتين
1.english: Indeed, it is Allah who is the [continual] Provider, the firm possessor of strength.
1.transliteration: Innal laaha Huwar Razzaaqu Zul Quwwatil Mateen

1.arabic: فإن للذين ظلموا ذنوبا مثل ذنوب أصحابهم فلا يستعجلون
1.english: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their predecessors, so let them not impatiently urge Me.
1.transliteration: Fa inna lillazeena zalamoo zanoobam misla zanoobi ashaabihim falaa yasta'jiloon

1.arabic: فويل للذين كفروا من يومهم الذي يوعدون
1.english: And woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.
1.transliteration: Fawailul lillazeena kafaroo miny yawmihimul lazee yoo'adoon
